mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in monthly employment growth signals weakening aggregate demand and rising recession probability, triggering risk-off repricing that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS three-month change falls below 50.0 thousand
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of housing starts and consumer credit growth indicates a synchronized pullback in durable goods spending and mortgage-driven wealth effects, which compresses corporate revenue expectations and depresses equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression controlling for GDP growth, or forward returns decouple from the joint signal in more than 40% of rolling 60-day windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth directly reflects near-term corporate capacity utilization and margin pressure; sustained output expansion signals strong operational cash flows that immediately translate into positive short-term equity momentum.
failure_condition: The signal's predictive power reverses during periods of supply chain disruption, or the 5-day forward return correlation with INDPRO month-over-month changes falls below 0.05 across three consecutive business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth decelerating below the historical trend rate of 2.5% triggers a repricing of earnings multiples as investors anticipate margin compression and inventory drawdowns, yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth rate falls below 2.5%
failure_condition: Walk-forward directional accuracy remains below 50% over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns do not exhibit a statistically significant negative mean when the threshold is breached.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous inflation acceleration and federal funds rate hikes compress real yields and increase the cost of capital, forcing equity risk premium expansion and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when real yields remain positive, or the interaction term's predictive IC drops below 0.02 over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month employment growth reinforces consumer spending power and business investment confidence, providing a continuous baseline of positive earnings momentum that supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The inverse relationship between unemployment changes and forward returns breaks down during structural labor market shifts, or the 5-day forward return correlation exceeds zero in more than 70% of declining unemployment months.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in industrial production below the 100 index level signals capacity underutilization and deflationary pressure, which erodes corporate pricing power and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral in more than 55% of instances where INDPRO breaches the threshold, or the signal's information ratio falls below 0.1 over a 5-year out-of-sample period.
